题干: For any complex number $z=x+iy$, $|e^z|=e^x$ always holds.
The function $\sin z$ is entire (analytic everywhere in $\mathbb{C}$).
The equation $\cos z=0$ has only real solutions $z=\pi/2+n\pi$ ($n$ is an integer) in the complex plane.
For any complex $z$, $\sin(iz)=i\sinh z$.
If $z=x+iy$, then $|\cos z|^2=\cos^2 x+\sinh^2 y$.
The tangent function $\tan z$ is $\pi$-periodic.
For any non-zero complex $z$, the multi-valued equality $\log(z^2)=2\log z$ holds as sets.
The principal logarithm satisfies $\mathrm{Log}(e^z)=z$ for all complex $z$.
The principal value $\mathrm{Log}(-1)=i\pi$.
For any non-zero complex $z$, $e^{\log z}=z$ (using the multi-valued logarithm).
Using the branch $L_{(-\pi)}$, we have $L_{(-\pi)}(-1)=i\pi$.
All possible values of $i^i$ are real numbers.
Under the principal branch, $(-1)^{\sqrt{2}}$ is a real number.
For any integer $n$, $e^{(2n+1)\pi i}=-1$.
The hyperbolic sine function $\sinh z$ is entire.
If $\sin z=0$, then $z=n\pi$ for some integer $n$.
For any complex $z$, $\cos(z+\pi)=-\cos z$.
The principal logarithm of $1+i$ equals $\ln(\sqrt{2})+i(\pi/4)$.
All values of the multi-valued logarithm $\log 1$ are $2k\pi i$ ($k$ is an integer).
As sets, $\log(1/z)=-\log z$ for any non-zero $z$.
Under the principal branch, the modulus of $(1-i)^i$ equals $e^{-\pi/4}$.
If $c$ is an integer, the complex power function $z^c$ is single-valued.
Under the principal branch, $\frac{d}{dz}(z^c)=c z^{c-1}$ holds for non-zero $z$.
All possible values of $1^{1/\pi}$ lie on the unit circle.
Using the branch $L_{(\pi/4)}$, we have $L_{(\pi/4)}(i^2)=2L_{(\pi/4)}(i)$.
For any complex $z$, $\sin^2 z+\cos^2 z=1$.
The singularities of $\cot z$ occur at $z=n\pi$ ($n$ is an integer).
The principal logarithm of $-i$ equals $-i\pi/2$.
For any non-zero $z$ and positive integer $n$, $\log(z^n)=n\log z$ as sets.
On the domain $D_\alpha=\{r e^{i\theta}: r>0,\ \alpha<\theta<\alpha+2\pi\}$, the branch $L_\alpha(z)=\ln r+i\theta$ is analytic.
